name: Chicken and Mushroom Crepes
servings: serves 4 | prep. time 10 minutes | cooking time 55 minutes

ingredients.item: Salt and black pepper to taste
Salt and black pepper to taste
Place the boneless chicken on a rack on a baking sheet, then roast for 40 minutes.
Allow the chicken to cool and shred it with a fork. Put it aside until crepes are made.
To make the crepes, mix flour with salt and milk in a bowl.
Stir in beaten egg, then mix well until smooth.
Add dill melted butter and dill, then mix well.
Oil a skillet and set it to medium-high heat.
Pour in 1 ladle of the prepared batter onto the skillet, spread this batter thinly, and cook for 2 minutes per side. Repeat until you have made 4 crepes.
Transfer the crepes to a working surface.
To make the filling, melt butter in the same skillet and stir in mushrooms, black pepper, salt, Worcestershire sauce, and parsley.
Sauté for 7 minutes, then transfer to a bowl.
Place 2 tablespoons of the mushroom mixture with ¼ of the shredded chicken and 2 tablespoons of sauce in each crepe and roll up and place in a greased cake pan or gratin dish.
To make the sauce, melt butter in a deep skillet, then add flour to make a roux.
Slowly add milk while stirring constantly.
Add black pepper, salt, and garlic clove, then mix until smooth with no lumps.
Drizzle remaining bechamel and cheese on top of rolled crepes.
